output.summary: Conditionally hide the isTall toggle in the editor and suppress isTall styling in PHP when the parent MasonryCards block has masonryStyle disabled. Both TextCard and ImageCard child blocks already consume the masonryCards/masonryStyle context -- the change is purely about gating the isTall UI controls and frontend class application on that context value.

fileChanges.description: Conditionally render isTall controls only when masonryStyle is true
fileChanges.details: 1. Wrap the BlockControls ToolbarButton (isTall toggle in toolbar) with a `{masonryStyle && ...}` conditional so it only renders when masonryStyle is enabled. 2. Wrap the InspectorControls PanelBody containing the 'Tall Card' ToggleControl with the same `{masonryStyle && ...}` conditional. 3. No changes to the className logic -- in the editor, isTall classes are already gated by `masonryStyle && isTall` for masonry-specific classes. The only unconditional isTall usages are `sm:min-h-[596px]` and the tall card visual split classes, which should also be gated: wrap them with `masonryStyle && isTall` instead of just `isTall`. Similarly for the title `md:pb-8` and content `md:pt-8` classes.

fileChanges.description: Suppress isTall styling when masonryStyle is false
fileChanges.details: Create a local variable `$is_tall = $masonry_style && $isTall;` near the top (after context extraction). Replace all occurrences of `$isTall` in the class_name arrays with `$is_tall`. This ensures that when masonryStyle is false, isTall has no visual effect on the frontend. Affected lines: `sm:min-h-[596px]` condition, the tall card background split condition, the title `md:pb-8` condition, and the content `md:pt-8` condition.

fileChanges.description: Conditionally render isTall controls only when masonryStyle is true
fileChanges.details: 1. Wrap the BlockControls ToolbarButton (isTall toggle in toolbar) with a `{masonryStyle && ...}` conditional. 2. Wrap the InspectorControls PanelBody for 'Card Settings' (containing the Tall Card ToggleControl) with the same conditional. 3. Gate the `sm:min-h-[596px]` class with `masonryStyle && isTall` instead of just `isTall`.

fileChanges.description: Suppress isTall styling when masonryStyle is false
fileChanges.details: Create a local variable `$is_tall = $masonry_style && $isTall;` near the top (after context extraction). Replace all occurrences of `$isTall` in the class_name array with `$is_tall`. Affected line: `sm:min-h-[596px]` condition.

designDecisions.decision: isTall attribute value is preserved in the saved data even when masonryStyle is off -- it is only hidden from the UI and suppressed in rendering. This means if a user sets isTall=true, then disables masonryStyle, then re-enables masonryStyle, the isTall value is remembered.
designDecisions.evidence: The user asked to hide the toggle and not apply isTall, not to reset/clear the attribute value. Preserving the value provides better UX when toggling masonryStyle on and off.

designDecisions.decision: In PHP, use a derived variable ($is_tall = $masonry_style && $isTall) rather than modifying each condition individually. This is cleaner and ensures consistent suppression across all isTall-dependent styles.
designDecisions.evidence: Both PHP templates already extract $masonry_style from context. A single derived variable is the simplest approach to gate all isTall behavior.
